response.justification: The candidate brings strong geospatial/GIS domain experience and a solid software-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, low-latency async systems), which partially aligns with the routing-tiling pipeline domain. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the candidate's C/C++ exposure is limited and dated (NI LabWindows/CVI components and a university thesis), with no Rust experience. Their recent and dominant stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, leaving the core language and navigation-routing pipeline requirements only weakly covered.
strengths: Extensive geospatial/GIS experience (GISCollective, Open Green Map, web GIS apps)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching, real-time metadata)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring
concerns: Modern C++ experience is limited and dated; no Rust experience
No direct navigation/routing data-pipeline or routing-tile production experience
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the primary stack
